pi_items.source: NCH Copper Point of Sale official website (https://www.nchsoftware.com/point-of-sale/index.html) states: 'Accept cash, checks, credit cards or even multiple payment types on any transaction.'
NCH Copper Point of Sale official website explicitly lists credit cards as a supported payment method.

NCH Copper Point of Sale official website explicitly lists checks (cheque) as a supported payment method. Also confirmed in other task descriptions (new_store_configuration task mentions Check as a payment method).

Task description states: 'add a NEW payment method named Store Credit'. This indicates Store Credit is not a built-in payment method but should be created during task execution. Cannot verify if this method actually exists or can be created in Copper POS without execution.

Web search of NCH Copper Point of Sale documentation reveals supported payment methods are: Cash, Checks, Credit Cards, and Debit Cards. EFTPOS (Electronic Funds Transfer at Point of Sale) is not listed as a discrete payment method in Copper POS. While the term EFTPOS is generic technology for card payments, it is not a selectable payment method in Copper's interface.
File path cannot be verified without executing the task. The verifier.py code references 'C:\tmp\task_result.json' (not payment_config.json) as the expected output file.

privileged_info_summary: NCH Copper Point of Sale officially supports four payment methods: Cash, Check, Credit Card, and Debit Card. These core methods are verified as built-in features. The task requires enabling Cash, Credit Card, and Check (all verified standard methods), plus adding a custom 'Store Credit' method. EFTPOS is not documented as a payment method in Copper POS—it is a generic term for card-payment technology rather than a discrete method selectable in the software.
